zotero.abstractNote: We present Paint-it, a novel framework for generating high-quality, physically-based textures for 3D meshes from text prompts. Existing methods for text-to-3D texture generation often suffer from view-inconsistency, baked-in lighting, or low-resolution textures. To address these issues, we propose a two-stage optimization process. First, we optimize a texture map using a pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model, leveraging a physically-based rendering (PBR) pipeline to ensure view-consistency and disentangle lighting from material properties. Second, we introduce a deep convolutional texture map optimization technique that refines the texture map by learning to predict high-frequency details and correcting artifacts. Our method produces high-resolution, PBR-ready textures that are consistent across views and free from baked-in lighting, significantly outperforming existing state-of-the-art approaches in both visual quality and geometric fidelity.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
